summary: Matched the ftCommon MissFoot action-state code and a nearby light-object animation callback by moving functions out of asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s into new C files. The PR added src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_MissFoot.c with ftCo_8009F39C and the MissFoot Anim/IASA/Phys/Coll callbacks, added src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_09F4.c for ftCo_8009F480, wired both new objects into obj_files.mk, and updated includes/prototypes. The matching report shows 100% for both new C files.
what_changed: Removed the assembly implementations of ftCo_8009F39C, ftCo_MissFoot_Anim, ftCo_MissFoot_IASA, ftCo_MissFoot_Phys, ftCo_MissFoot_Coll, and the local label .L_8009F480 from asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s.
Added ftCo_MissFoot.c implementing the MissFoot state entry and callbacks in C.
Added ftCo_09F4.c implementing ftCo_8009F480, a callback that calls HSD_LObjAnimAll on gobj->hsd_obj.
Changed the remaining assembly in ft_08A4.s to reference ftCo_8009F480 as a global symbol instead of the former local label .L_8009F480.
Moved the sdata2 constants used by ftCo_8009F39C out of the asm file by expressing them as C literals: y velocity 0, animation start 0, animation speed 1, blend/arg 0, and NULL.
Added headers ftCo_MissFoot.h and ftCo_09F4.h with address-commented prototypes and ftCommon forward declarations.
Added the two new C objects to TEXT_O_FILES in obj_files.mk before the remaining ft_08A4.s object.
Removed the old generic prototype unk_t ftCo_8009F39C(void) from src/melee/ft/ft_08A4.h.
smart_moves: Kept the partially decompiled ft_08A4.s file buildable by promoting the former internal label .L_8009F480 into a C function symbol and updating the asm callsite to use ftCo_8009F480@ha/@l.
Split the tiny light-object callback ftCo_8009F480 into its own ftCo_09F4.c unit instead of forcing it into the MissFoot file, matching the address/file partition shown by the report.
Used typed fighter access through ftCo_Fighter* fp = gobj->user_data, which made the assembly's offset writes/readable control flow become fp->x8c_kb_vel.y = 0 and a ground_or_air check.
Replaced unknown or broad declarations with action-specific headers under ftCommon, improving include locality for future ftCommon matching.
Preserved function ordering and object ordering so the new C code could replace only the matched assembly range while leaving later ft_08A4.s code intact.
decomp_lessons: For small action-state handlers, the entry function often maps cleanly to: clear a fighter field, call Fighter_ChangeMotionState with an enum motion-state ID, call shared helper routines, then conditionally call another helper based on fighter state.
An empty IASA callback can be expressed as an empty C function body when the assembly is just blr.
Simple forwarding callbacks such as Phys and Coll can match as one-line wrappers around shared physics/collision helpers, here ft_80084DB0 and ft_80082F28.
When decompiling out of a mixed asm file, any remaining asm that referenced a local label must be retargeted to the new global C symbol.
C literals can eliminate sdata2 constants from the asm file when the generated code matches; in this PR, NULL/0.0 and 1.0 constants associated with MissFoot were no longer needed in ft_08A4.s.
Adding new C files to obj_files.mk in the same relative position as the removed asm region is part of the matching work, not just cleanup.
naming_conventions: Common fighter action-state files use the ftCo_ prefix and action name, e.g. ftCo_MissFoot.c and ftCo_MissFoot.h.
Action-state callback names follow ftCo_<Action>_Anim, ftCo_<Action>_IASA, ftCo_<Action>_Phys, and ftCo_<Action>_Coll.
Unidentified address-based functions retained numeric names such as ftCo_8009F39C and ftCo_8009F480.
Header guards in this slice used GALE01 address-style names, e.g. GALE01_09CD5C and GALE01_09F480.
Headers kept address comments before prototypes, e.g. /* 09CD5C */ void ftCo_8009F39C(ftCo_GObj* gobj);
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Matched ftCo_8009F39C by assigning gobj->user_data to a local fighter pointer, clearing fp->x8c_kb_vel.y, then calling Fighter_ChangeMotionState(gobj, ftCo_MS_MissFoot, Ft_MF_None, 0, 1, 0, NULL).
Matched the post-motion-state setup by calling ftCommon_8007D468(fp), then calling ftCommon_8007D5D4(fp) only when fp->ground_or_air == GA_Ground.
Matched ftCo_MissFoot_Phys and ftCo_MissFoot_Coll as direct wrappers around ft_80084DB0(gobj) and ft_80082F28(gobj).
Promoted the asm-local .L_8009F480 callback to void ftCo_8009F480(ftCo_GObj* gobj) and changed the existing HSD_GObjProc_8038FD54 setup in asm to pass ftCo_8009F480.
Used gobj->hsd_obj for the load from offset 0x28 before HSD_LObjAnimAll in ftCo_8009F480.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or type ftCo_8009F39C or ftCo_8009F480.
Search for other ftCommon action-state PRs that use the Anim/IASA/Phys/Coll callback pattern.
Check whether ftCo_09F4.h header guard GALE01_09F480 or static comment on ftCo_8009F480 was later cleaned up.
Look for subsequent decompilation of the remaining ft_08A4.s functions around ftCo_8009F4A4.
